items.headline: City launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help businesses at local shopping centers get compliant with licensing requirements without penalties. This could affect small businesses and retailers operating in the city's commercial areas.

items.headline: New economic incentives coming for retail and small businesses
items.summary: The city council is considering new incentive programs to attract and support retail shops, small businesses, and startups in Cupertino. Details on what these incentives entail should be clarified at the March 27 meeting.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing development approved at East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View City Council voted to approve a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading, moving it toward final approval. This project will add housing and likely commercial or office space to that location.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for California's Prohousing Incentive Program Round 4, seeking state grants to support local housing development. This could bring money to the city for affordable or workforce housing projects.

items.headline: City seeks public input on how to spend HUD housing grants
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting comments on its draft plan for federal housing funds for 2026-27. This is your chance to influence how the city uses federal money for affordable housing and related programs.

items.headline: Safe parking program community meeting held
items.summary: Sunnyvale held a public meeting on March 23 about its safe parking program, which typically provides designated lots where people living in vehicles can park overnight with city support and services. This suggests the city is either expanding, adjusting, or seeking community input on this homelessness initiative.
